attributes.description: Alerts when `memcached.connections.current > 800`. Approaching the `-c` limit means new connections will be refused.
attributes.name: [Memcached OTel] High connection count

investigation_guide.blob:
## Memcached high connection count

### What fired
Current connections exceeded 800 during the window.

### Why it matters
Memcached has a fixed connection limit (`-c`, default 1024). Above ~80% of the limit, new connections can be refused silently. Usually caused by client-side connection churn or missing pooling.

### Triage
1. Check client connection churn with `connections.total` rate.
2. Inspect client pool sizing.

### Remediation
- Enable/tune client-side pooling.
- Raise `-c` if legitimately needed.

### Tuning
- 800 assumes 1024 limit; tune to your `-c` setting.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-memcachedreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE memcached.connections.current IS NOT NULL
// Current connections (gauge) — use max over window for peak detection
| STATS max_connections = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(memcached.connections.current))
  BY host.name, COALESCE(service.instance.id, host.name)
// Alert when connections exceed 800 — adjust for your -c limit
| WHERE max_connections > 800
| SORT max_connections DESC
| LIMIT 20
